channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Reuben Styles of Peking Duk reflects on tragic Braidwood crash and road safety advocacy
item.description: Reuben Styles, one-half of Canberra-born electronic duo Peking Duk, has opened up about a tragic car crash in Braidwood that deeply affected him during his teenage years. Although he was not in the ve...

item.title: Sindh removes fixed closing hours for markets and businesses to support economy
item.description: On Saturday, Sindh Senior Minister Sharjeel Memon announced that the provincial government has removed fixed closing times for shops, markets, shopping malls, hotels, restaurants, food outlets, marria...

item.title: Trump Visits China Amid Global Tensions but Domestic Challenges Persist
item.description: President Donald Trump has returned to China for the first time in nearly ten years, receiving grand ceremonies and high-level hospitality from Chinese President Xi Jinping. The visit includes a milit...

item.title: Practical Tips for Gardening in Limited Spaces
item.description: Gardening in small spaces, such as apartments, condos, or homes with limited outdoor areas, can be both enjoyable and productive with the right approach. By utilising vertical space through wall plant...

item.title: Olakau’atu stars as Manly defeat under-strength Wests Tigers 46-18
item.description: Manly Sea Eagles delivered a dominant performance against the injury-hit Wests Tigers during the Magic Round, securing a 46-18 win at Suncorp Stadium. Haumole Olakau’atu, in what could be his last aud...

item.title: President Marcos Urges PMA Graduates to Serve the Nation with Integrity, Not Politics
item.description: During the commencement exercises of the Philippine Military Academy (PMA) Talang Dangal Class of 2026 held in Baguio City, President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. reminded the new graduates that th...

item.title: Big Bite 2026: Showcasing Cagayan de Oro’s Diverse Culinary Offerings
item.description: Big Bite 2026, held at Ayala Malls Centrio in Cagayan de Oro, celebrates the city’s vibrant and evolving food culture. From traditional Filipino flavors to international cuisines, the event highlights...

item.title: Alberta’s Ukrainian Cultural Heritage Village Reopens Following 2025 Fire
item.description: The Ukrainian Cultural Heritage Village, located east of Edmonton, Alberta, is reopening to visitors on May 16, 2026, after being forced to close for the 2025 season due to a devastating grass fire. T...

item.title: Tanzania advocates for citizen-focused tech policies at Geneva AI meeting
item.description: Tanzania recently participated in a high-level two-day meeting on AI governance organized by the International Telecommunication Union (ITU) in Geneva. The country was represented by the Minister for ...

item.title: CJI Surya Kant Criticises Frivolous Petitions and Unemployed Youth Engaging in Activism
item.description: Chief Justice of India, Surya Kant, on Friday made controversial remarks in the Supreme Court, describing some unemployed youngsters as 'cockroaches'. He observed that those without employment often t...

item.title: Kiharu MP Proposes Legal Changes to Bring Down Fuel Prices in Kenya
item.description: Kiharu MP Ndindi Nyoro has taken steps to address the high fuel prices affecting Kenyans following the latest increase by the Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (EPRA). In a letter to the Natio...

item.title: Maharashtra engineer killed by lover and accomplices, body dumped in green drum
item.description: In a shocking incident from Vasai, Maharashtra, 26-year-old engineer Arbaz Maqsood Ali Khan was brutally murdered by his married lover, her husband, and two accomplices after he allegedly stopped send...

item.title: FoodForward SA Supports Pregnant Women and Young Children in Vulnerable Western and Eastern Cape Communities
item.description: In the Western and Eastern Cape, FoodForward SA is making a tangible difference through its mother-and-child nutrition programme, which focuses on pregnant women and children under the age of five in ...

item.title: Unavailable: Unable to access content
item.description: The article could not be accessed due to a server restriction, resulting in an 'Access Denied' error message. Therefore, the specific content, including the images and descriptions featured in the new...

item.title: Min Woo Lee contends strongly after second-round performance at PGA Championship
item.description: Australian golfer Min Woo Lee has positioned himself well to challenge for his first major title after a resilient second round at the PGA Championship at Aronimink Golf Club, Pennsylvania. Despite ch...

item.title: UK and European countries agree on declaration to streamline migration removals
item.description: The UK and 45 other European countries have signed a declaration at a summit in Moldova aimed at making it easier to deport illegal migrants. The agreement encourages national courts to handle most mi...

item.title: Meghan Markle attends Lost Screen Memorial in Geneva to advocate for stronger online child protection
item.description: The Duchess of Sussex, Meghan Markle, made a surprise visit to Geneva, Switzerland, this past weekend to advocate for enhanced protections for children online. She attended the inauguration of the Los...

item.title: Government boosts youth and women participation in livestock and fisheries through BBT projects and loans
item.description: Tanzania government is making serious moves to modernize its livestock and fisheries sectors by focusing on youth and women empowerment through the Building a Better Tomorrow (BBT) initiative. Under t...

item.title: Pakistan, Egypt agree to boost cooperation on security, counter-terrorism and cybercrime
item.description: On Saturday, Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi held a meeting with a delegation from Egypt to discuss strengthening cooperation in multiple security-related areas, including counter-terrorism, cybercrime...

item.title: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr reflects on his tenure and controversial actions
item.description: FCC Chairman Brendan Carr, appointed by former President Donald Trump in January 2025, recently reflected on his legacy in an interview with The Financial Times. Carr expressed a somber view, acknowle...
